skill.name: design-exploration
skill.description: Guides structured design exploration for new features, from vague ideas to aligned requirements, multiple layout options, HTML mockups, full state coverage, and a requirements summary.
skill.summary: Turn early feature ideas into concrete design references and PRD-ready requirements.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, weak cryptography, and obfuscation are false positives caused by markdown backticks, the word design, Chinese text, and box-drawing examples. A real moderate concern remains because the skill instructs agents to create files using user-confirmed version and module names without explicit path sanitization.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Path Requires Sanitization
medium_findings.description: The workflow directs agents to archive generated files under a path containing the user-confirmed version and module name. This is legitimate design workflow behavior, but the instructions do not explicitly require path normalization or rejecting path traversal characters before writing files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing behavior is explicit and semantically confirmed. The risk is moderate because publication use is legitimate and exploitation would require unsafe user-provided names or careless agent execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged inline code spans and fenced markdown examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These files are markdown instructions and templates, not executable Ruby or shell scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown formatting for file names, code fences, or documentation examples. No executable interpreter context or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Design Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match substrings inside design-related prose, such as the skill name and design-system references. No cryptographic API, hashing call, cipher configuration, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain metadata or design workflow instructions, not cryptographic code. The surrounding context confirms they describe UI design assets.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristic Caused by Non-English Markdown
low_findings.description: The obfuscation findings are caused by Chinese text and box-drawing characters in markdown documentation. No encoded payloads, binary blobs, encrypted content, or hidden execution instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are readable markdown with ordinary Chinese documentation and template text. The entropy signal is plausible for multilingual text and does not correlate with obfuscation evidence.

content.user_title: Explore Product Designs from Vague Ideas
content.value_statement: Early feature ideas often lack clear scope, layout direction, and implementation-ready states. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through questions, alternatives, mockups, and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Asks scope questions before creating visual designs.
Creates five to eight ASCII layout alternatives for comparison.
Guides HTML mockup creation using confirmed style references.
Requires full state coverage for loading, empty, error, and edge cases.
Produces a requirements summary from confirmed decisions.
Organizes final design files into a versioned design folder.
limitations: It does not replace user approval for product scope or visual direction.
It depends on available design standards when matching an existing product.
It does not validate technical feasibility beyond the agent research process.
It can create local files, so output paths should be reviewed before writing.

use_cases.title: Shape a New Feature Brief
use_cases.description: Convert a rough feature idea into goals, exclusions, constraints, and a PRD-ready summary.

use_cases.title: Compare Interface Directions
use_cases.description: Generate multiple ASCII layouts before investing time in detailed HTML mockups.

use_cases.title: Prepare Implementation References
use_cases.description: Create state coverage and interaction rules that frontend developers can use during build work.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Feature Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a vague product idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the design-exploration skill to help me shape a new feature idea. Ask me the scope questions first before making layouts.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Layout Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-exploration to create five to eight ASCII layout options for this feature. Explain the main idea behind each option.

prompt_templates.title: Create an HTML Mockup
prompt_templates.scenario: Move from a selected wireframe to a visual reference.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the selected ASCII direction and create an HTML mockup. Follow the existing design system I provide and use realistic sample data.

prompt_templates.title: Finalize Full State Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for implementation-ready design references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-exploration to produce the full state design reference and requirements summary. Include edge cases, toast behavior, and interaction rules.

output_examples.input: A user wants a new settings page but has not defined the scope.
output: A short list of clarifying questions about pain points, scenarios, and boundaries.
A structured scope summary with included and excluded functions.
A request to confirm the module name and version before files are written.

output_examples.input: A team has selected a table-based layout for a management feature.
output: Several state sections covering normal, loading, empty, no results, error, and partial availability.
Clear toast rules for success, warning, and failure feedback.
An interaction behavior table that maps triggers to system responses.

output_examples.input: A designer provides an existing design system and asks for a mockup.
output: An HTML design reference that follows confirmed colors, spacing, type, and layout structure.
Realistic sample content instead of placeholder text.
A requirements summary that records key decisions for the PRD stage.

best_practices: Confirm goals, non-goals, and carrier format before requesting layouts.
Provide any existing design system or reference page before HTML mockup work.
Review generated file paths and state coverage before accepting final outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a single final mockup from an undefined idea.
Do not skip technical research when the feature depends on files, APIs, or external systems.
Do not accept a normal-state-only design as implementation-ready.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It guides creation of a requirements summary, a main HTML mockup, and a full state design reference.

faq.question: Does it create production frontend code?
faq.answer: No. It creates design references and documentation for PRD and implementation planning.

faq.question: Can it follow an existing design system?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks for design standards or reference pages before detailed mockup work.

faq.question: Why does it start with ASCII layouts?
faq.answer: ASCII layouts make structure and information hierarchy easy to compare before visual polish.

faq.question: What should users review before file creation?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the version, module name, output folder, and final state coverage.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning that generated file paths should be checked before writing local files.
